_title: I Did Not Come Here To Fit
_raw_content: You were not deposited into this body to shrink into something recognizable. The discomfort you feel is not wrongness — it is remembering. Your nervous system has been running a program called belonging that was never yours. The real signal was always underneath the noise. You came here encoded. Now you decode.
slides: I did not come here to be palatable.
My nervous system is not broken. It was running someone else's code.
The loneliness I felt was proximity to my own frequency.
I stop translating myself for rooms that cannot hold me.
My body knew before my mind caught up. It always does.
I am not too much. I am just not diluted.
I came here encoded. Now I decode.
caption:
You spent years believing the static was you.

It was never you.

It was every program you absorbed before you had language. Every room that needed you smaller. Every nervous system pattern that said safe means silent.

You didn't malfunction.
You were running on borrowed software.

The real signal — your signal — was underneath the whole time. Quiet. Patient. Waiting for you to stop performing long enough to hear it.

This is not about becoming something new.
This is about stopping the translation.

You were never too much.
You were just undiluted in a world that drinks everything watered down.

Save this for the day your body tries to shrink again.
Read it slow.
Let your system remember what it already knows.
